body.title: POST inventory — centralize existing boot-time self-checks
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Igor already runs implicit Power-On Self-Test activity at boot (venv/dep checks, DB connect, migrations, cortex init, milieu banner, subsystem imports, brainstem tick 0, and pe_chain pre-flight pytest mid-ticket). These are scattered and the boot console doesn't surface them as a single tree. This ticket inventories what's already happening, centralizes the logging, and exposes a single boot-time PASS/FAIL summary.

Framing (Akien 2026-04-23): not about adding new POST checks — those come later once we can see the tree. This is: make the existing ones legible, gather them centrally, then look at the whole system and ask 'what else should be a POST item?' Inside Igor, POST becomes another tree gathering disparate things.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint. Likely new `wild_igor/igor/system/post.py` aggregator + small edits at each existing check-point to report into it (install/bootstrap, main.py boot banner, cognition/__init__, memory/init, brainstem init, pe_chain pre-flight).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first. no-sqlite doesn't apply. Observer pattern from T-list-primitive is conceptually related but this ticket predates it — POST posts to a plain collector for now.

**Scope boundary:** IN — audit existing boot-time self-checks, route their results through one aggregator, emit a single PASS/FAIL tree to console + cc_inbox at boot completion. OUT — adding new POST checks (reply pipeline probe, consult probe, cortex roundtrip, UC handshake) — those are proposed follow-ups once the tree is visible.

**Test plan:** unit tests on the POST aggregator (register/report/summarize). Integration: boot Igor with a deliberately broken subsystem (e.g. bad OR key), verify POST tree shows FAIL at the right node and reports via the summary line.

**Sequence:** after the current engram + consult + web-reply + cc-inbox batch lands. Not gated on anything technical, just sequencing.
body.approved_plan: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.approval_notes: false-positive brainstem/core_patterns.py gate — ticket does not touch HIGH-inertia files; cleared 2026-04-23
